remarks.text: Save the Address, Save the Cloud (KubeVirt VM Migration Story)
remarks.content: Kubernetes is built for containers, and it’s been doing that since it used to run docker as an engine for its containers. But what if you want to add VMs to the mix? After all,... The post Save the Address, Save the Cloud (KubeVirt VM Migration Story) appeared first on Tigera – Creator of Cali
remarks.contentTranslation: Kubernetes是为容器构建的，自从它曾经将docker作为容器的引擎运行以来，它一直在这样做。但是，如果您想将虚拟机添加到组合中，该怎么办？毕竟，...帖子Save the Address, Save the Cloud （ KubeVirt虚拟机迁移故事）首先出现在Tigera – Cali的创建者

remarks.text: Harness Engineering for Self-Improvement
remarks.content: The concept of recursive self-improvement (RSI) dates back to I. J. Good (1965), where he defined an “ultraintelligent machine” as a system that can surpass humans in all intellectual activities and design better machines to improve itself. Yudkowsky (2008) used the phrase “recursi
remarks.contentTranslation: 递归自我提升（ RSI ）的概念可以追溯到I. J. Good （ 1965 ） ，他将“超智能机器”定义为能够在所有智力活动中超越人类并设计更好的机器来提升自身的系统。Yudkowsky （ 2008 ）使用了短语“recursi
remarks.translation: 自我提升的线束工程

remarks.role: Codex & ChatGPT OpenAI
remarks.text:
You know OpenAI is cooking when the sushi and tacos orders pile at the entrance of the office at 11pm. 

Bizarrely we need to eat to cook.
remarks.translation: 晚上11点，当办公室入口处的寿司和玉米饼订单堆积如山时，您知道OpenAI正在烹饪。奇怪的是，我们需要吃东西来做饭。

remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text: When do we stop comparing autonomous agents to hand-written code? You don’t see compilers comparing to engineers hand-writing assembly. https://t.co/YQBIPn8Eux
remarks.translation: 我们何时停止将自主Agent与手写代码进行比较？与工程师手写汇编相比，您看不到编译器。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The latest AI models being dropped are getting insanely good handling complex knowledge worker tasks, and especially dealing with sophisticated domains of work like legal, professional services, healthcare, and more. Grok 4.5 is another great entry here, especially on cost + performance. 

As models get much better at coding, math, reasoning, and are trained on a variety of key verticals, we’re going to see more leaps in what you can do with enterprise data and documents.
remarks.translation:
最新的AI模型正在被淘汰，它们在处理复杂的知识工作者任务方面表现得非常出色，尤其是在法律、专业服务、医疗保健等复杂工作领域。Grok 4.5是另一个伟大的进入，特别是在成本和性能方面。
随着模型在编码、数学、推理方面变得越来越好，并接受了各种关键垂直领域的培训，我们将在企业数据和文档方面看到更多的飞跃。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Steinberger
remarks.role: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world.
remarks.text:
OpenAI hired me, not OpenClaw.
The OpenClaw Foundation is independent, with sponsors rather than owners - and, for the first time, a full-time team keeping the claw alive and stable. 🦞

Couldn’t have done it without Dave and the team. Kudos! https://t.co/u0ZXGwydxw
remarks.translation: OpenAI雇佣了我，而不是OpenClaw。OpenClaw基金会是独立的，拥有赞助商而不是所有者，并且首次拥有一支全职团队，以保持利爪的活力和稳定性🦞。如果没有Dave和团队，就不可能做到这一点。谢谢！
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
Talked to a super agent-pilled founder. He bought Codex Max for all team members, and everybody pretty much gets all work done thru talking to their Codex all day

The side effect: people stopped talking to each other. Meetings are cancelled, collaboration is minimal, and team culture got worse

So much of enterprise AI usage is single-player now. What do we risk sacrificing when team members all talk to their agents instead of each other?

We need to move beyond human-human collaboration AND beyond human-agent collaboration to human-human-agent collaboration
remarks.translation:
与超级特工的创始人交谈。他为所有团队成员购买了Codex Max ，几乎所有人都通过整天与他们的Codex交谈来完成所有工作。副作用：人们不再互相交谈。会议被取消，协作很少，团队文化变得更糟。现在很多企业AI的使用都是单人游戏。当团队成员都与他们的Agent交谈而不是彼此交谈时，我们会冒什么牺牲的风险？
我们需要超越人与人之间的协作，超越人与人之间的协作，转向人与人之间的协作
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
New in Claude Code: /checkup

Run /checkup to:

1. Clean up unused skills/MCPs/plugins and save context
2. Dedup your local CLAUDE.md against the checked in CLAUDE.md
3. Break up root CLAUDE.md into nested CLAUDE.md's + skills
4. Turn off slow hooks
5. Update your Claude Code to the latest version
6. Enable auto mode by default
7. Pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands

.. And a few other goodies.

/checkup confirms with you before making any changes. Enjoy!
remarks.translation: Claude Code中的新功能：/checkup运行/checkup以： 1.清理未使用的技能/MCP/插件并保存上下文2.根据已签入的CLAUDE.md删除本地CLAUDE.md 3.将根CLAUDE.md分解为嵌套的CLAUDE.md的+技能4.关闭慢钩5.将您的Claude Code更新到最新版本6.默认情况下启用自动模式7.预先批准经常被拒绝的只读命令..以及其他一些好处。/检查在进行任何更改之前与您确认。祝您玩得开心！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Thariq
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai.   prev YC W20, southpkcommons, medialab
remarks.text:
this should be a huge update in your model of software engineering: rewrites can be good, cheap and fast

of course most apps are not as testable and verifiable as Bun, but the models will continue to get better at filling those gaps https://t.co/LjygnPM31O
remarks.translation: 这应该是软件工程模型的重大更新：重写可以是好的、便宜的和快速的，当然大多数应用程序都不像BUN那样可测试和可验证，但模型将继续更好地填补这些空白

remarks.name: Cat Wu
remarks.role: claude code + cowork anthropicai, prev: dagster, scale_ai
remarks.text:
Tomorrow at 10am PT I'm hosting a live walkthrough of how we progressed from single-player Claude Code to multi-player Claude Tag. Then, we're going deep on how Claude Tag actually works.

AI used to finish your sentence. Then, it wrote entire features. Now, Claude Tag can monitor your channels, do proactive work for you, the whole team can steer it, and it remembers what you told it last week.

Register: https://t.co/D8Lz61BXn4
remarks.translation: 明天太平洋时间上午10点，我将主持一次现场演练，讲述我们如何从单人游戏Claude Code发展到多人游戏Claude Tag。然后，我们将深入了解Claude Tag的实际工作原理。AI用来完成你的句子。然后，它写下了整个功能。现在， Claude Tag可以监控您的频道，为您积极主动地工作，整个团队都可以引导它，它会记住您上周告诉它的内容。注册：

Power law in action!

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.translation: 不要浪费时间。雄心勃勃。@ southpkcommons创始人奖学金应用程序将于8月2日到期。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
Every founder I meet worries about missing this moment.

The best worry about wasting it. There’s a difference.

It’s a waste to ignore how much the world has changed. It’s a waste to think you can capture value with pure software the same as 5 years ago.

It’s a waste to build something small.

We have seen the shift slowly and then very quickly at @southpkcommons.

Here’s who we want in the Founder Fellowship now: hardware tinkerers, mad scientists, obsessives, biohackers, people who build nuclear reactors in their basements.

People who want to get their hands dirty and touch grass and atoms.

If you are only building software, then please (for your own sake!) have a thesis that all your friends laugh at you about. Heresy is the price of ambition.

Then put yourself in the right environment to maximize your ambition.

(Apply by August 2nd)
remarks.translation:
我遇到的每位创始人都担心错过这一刻。最好的担心是浪费它。这是有区别的。忽视世界的变化是浪费。认为您可以像5年前那样使用纯软件来获取价值，这是一种浪费。建造一些小东西是浪费的。我们在@ southpkcommons看到了这种缓慢而迅速的变化。
这就是我们现在想要加入创始人奖学金的人：硬件修补匠、疯狂的科学家、痴迷者、生物黑客、在地下室建造核反应堆的人。想弄脏双手，触摸草地和原子的人。如果您只是在构建软件，那么（为了您自己！ ）请写一篇您的所有朋友都会嘲笑您的论文。异端是野心的代价。然后，将自己置于合适的环境中，以最大限度地实现您的抱负。
（ 8月2日前申请）
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 90: AI Pioneer Jürgen Schmidhuber on the State of AI Today
podcasts.excerpt: Dr. Jürgen Schmidhuber, a renowned scientist and AI researcher widely regarded as one of the pioneers in the field, originated key ideas behind today's transformers, LSTMs, and recursive self-improvement through his lab's work. He argues that true AGI remains bottlenecked by physical hardware, that today's AI data center investments are headed for a correction as open-source keeps pace with closed labs, and that the path to general intelligence runs through artificial curiosity and self-generated experimentation rather than internet data. He closes by reconsidering mainstream AI safety arguments and offers a sweeping vision of self-replicating robot societies eventually colonizing the solar system. (0:00) Intro (1:24) How Close Is Superhuman AI? (2:27) Why ChatGPT Didn't Surprise Him (3:21) The Path to Recursive Self-Improvement (9:01) Will AI Takeoff…

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: AI Vibe Check: Lab Wars, Why APIs Might Vanish & Future Predictions
podcasts.excerpt: Six months after their last roundup, Jacob sits down with Ari Morcos (Datology AI CEO, former Meta AI researcher) and Rob Toews (Radical Ventures partner, Forbes AI columnist) to take stock of an AI landscape that has shifted dramatically: coding agents crossing the long-time-horizon threshold has turned engineers into managers of agents, near-frontier open weight AI looks like it may be disappearing as Meta and the Chinese labs pull back, and Anthropic's restrictions on its newly released Fable model have its biggest supporters questioning whether safety framing is masking competitive positioning. The conversation runs through the full state of the lab wars, including Rob doubling down on his Sam Altman ouster prediction and the Bret Taylor succession theory, why Google's structural advantages remain intact despite falling behind on coding, what xAI's Cursor acquisition is really for, and Ari'…
